title: acer cappadocicum subsp. catalpifolium
intro:
Acer cappadocicum subsp. catalpifolium is a subspecies of the Acer cappadocicum maple tree, native to the mountainous regions of China, characterized by its unique combination of broad, heart-shaped leaves and a broad, spreading habit.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily its bark, which contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Acer cappadocicum subsp. catalpifolium include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or decoction, or used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and infusions.
